I. Information Science.question: ________is a person who works in information centers, in information science, and museums.
A systematic arrangement of entries designed to enable users to locate information in a document.
Which one of the following is correct about the nature of information except_____________?
options: A. Information can exist in a liquid state
C. Information can exist in the gesture
B. Information can exist in a solid-state
D. Information only flows from source to receiver
I. Information Science.answer: D. Information only flows from source to receiver

I. Information Science.question: Which one of the following is the Function of information system___________?
________ is the design of organization, labeling, navigation, and search systems to help people find and manage information more successfully.
Any law, regulation, rule, or practice (written or unwritten) that affects the creation, acquisition, organization, and dissemination of information.
Which Characteristics of information defined the value and usefulness of information is highly subjective, because what is information for one person may not be for another.
An interdisciplinary science primarily concerned with the analysis, collection, classification, manipulation, storage, retrieval, and dissemination of information.

options: B. Give your data via email

VII. Information Retrieval System.question: Which of the following steps of in the process of constructing indexing file are performed in the right order?
options: A. Tokenization, Stemming, Stop-word Removal, Thesaurus Construction
B. Stop-word Removal, Tokenization, Thesaurus Construction, Stemming
C. Tokenization, Stop-word Removal, Stemming, Thesaurus Construction
D. Stemming, Thesaurus Construction, Tokenization, Stop-word Removal
VII. Information Retrieval System.answer: C. Tokenization, Stop-word Removal, Stemming, Thesaurus Construction

VII. Information Retrieval System.question: One of the following statements is true about Zipf’s law in statistical properties of a text?
options: A. The numerical position of a word in a list sorted by decreasing frequency
B. Word significance is very useful for indexing
C. Concerns the size of the number of unique words in the corpus
D. Find documents relevant to an information need from a large document set
VII. Information Retrieval System.answer: A. The numerical position of a word in a list sorted by decreasing frequency

VII. Information Retrieval System.question: Which one of the following is a non-binary term weighting mechanism that refers the sum of term frequencies of a given term across the document collection or corpus?
______________ is a subfield of  information retrieval dealing with retrieving information written in a language different from the language of the user's query.

Which one of the following operations is becomes the first and the last step in process of building text information retrieval system respectively?
options: A. Define a text database and Users specifies their need
B. Build an index and weighting a term
C. Rank of relevant documents and Text operations
D. Define a text database and Users reformulate query
VII. Information Retrieval System.answer: D. Define a text database and Users reformulate query

VII. Information Retrieval System.question: One of the following is the stemming technique that works using set of rules in information retrieval system?
The TF-IDF weight a given term is best t will be ______ when t occurs in the document but rarely in the rest of the document collection.
Which of the following is not true about suffix array index structure in information retrieval system?
options: A. Suffix array is always sorted
B. Suffix array is used in string matching problem
C. Suffix array is always unsorted
D. Suffix array contains all the suffixes of the given string
VII. Information Retrieval System.answer: C. Suffix array is always unsorted

IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.question: Of the following alternatives, one is not true about the database approach
options: A. It has a crash recovery mechanism
B. It is easy to apply integrity constraint
C. It helps to store a collection of raw data files on the hard disk.
D. It has a backup and recovery mechanism
IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.answer: C. It helps to store a collection of raw data files on the hard disk.

IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.question: Suppose, after your graduation, you will be recruited by the Commercial Bank of Ethiopia with the job title of Database Administrator. Which one of the following alternatives is your responsibility?
options: A. Choosing the appropriate structures to represent and store data
B. Authorizing access to the database
C. Determine the requirements of end users and develop specifications for canned transactions
S. modules and interfaces as a package
IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.answer: B. Authorizing access to the database

IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.question: Of the following alternatives, one is false about DBMS Architecture.
options: A. In the local database system, all the processing is done on a single server
S. Architecture, all layers are independent
C. As the number of layer increase, security, data integrity, and scalability will increase
D. In the second-tier Architecture, the application layer is responsible for load balancing, query request correctness, and security
IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.answer: D. In the second-tier Architecture, the application layer is responsible for load balancing, query request correctness, and security

Which condition should be satisfied for computing union operation?
options: A. Both the relations have different numbers of attributes and the data types of their corresponding attributes must be different
B. Both the relations have different numbers of attributes and the data types of their corresponding attributes must be the same
C. Both the relations have the same number of attributes and the data types of their corresponding attributes must be different
D. Both the relations have the same number of attributes and the data types of their corresponding attributes must be different
IV. Fundamental of Database Questions.answer: D. Both the relations have the same number of attributes and the data types of their corresponding attributes must be different
